summary: Large stage decompilation PR for Rainbow Cruise grrcruise, adding data declarations, typed ground state usage, headers, includes, and multiple recovered or improved grRCruise functions. Bot report records 6 new matches and 11 unmatched-item improvements in main/melee/gr/grrcruise, with no human review comments present in the slice.

key_files.role: Primary decompilation target; adds grRc stage data, many includes, typed state access, recovered functions, and matching tactics.
evidence_refs: changed_files: src/melee/gr/grrcruise.c added 716 deleted 33 hunks 12
diff_excerpt: grrcruise.c adds S16Vec3 grRc_803E4DA8 and struct StageData grRc_803E4ECC
diff_excerpt: grrcruise.c replaces several /// #grRCruise stubs with C bodies including grRCruise_801FF924, 801FFADC, 80200154, 80200578, 8020071C, 80200C04
text_corpus issue_comment: bot report lists grrcruise new matches and improvements

key_files.role: Ground/stage type updates needed by grrcruise typed field access.
evidence_refs: changed_files: src/melee/gr/types.h added 37 deleted 2 hunks 7
metadata initial_classification includes types, Ground, GroundVars-style search terms

key_files.role: Header surface adjusted for grrcruise declarations.
evidence_refs: changed_files: src/melee/gr/grrcruise.h added 2 deleted 2 hunks 1

key_files.role: Exports grAnime_801C7BA0 for use by grrcruise code.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: adds void grAnime_801C7BA0(HSD_GObj*, int, u32, float) to granime.h

key_files.role: Removes stale local static declaration for grAnime_801C7BA0 after header export.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: removes static grAnime_801C7BA0 declaration from granime.c
changed_files: src/melee/gr/granime.c added 0 deleted 2 hunks 1

what_changed: Added Rainbow Cruise stage data in grrcruise.c, including grRc_803E4DA8 S16Vec3 entries and grRc_803E4ECC StageData for RCRUISE and /GrRc.dat.
Expanded grRc_804D6A10 from a one-field placeholder into a local struct with float and integer fields used by recovered grrcruise behavior.
Recovered or improved multiple grRCruise functions, including initialization/setup, scroll/camera movement, collision response, rotation logic, and state-machine updates.
Changed grAnime_801C7BA0 from a grAnime.c-local declaration to a public declaration in granime.h so grrcruise can call it.
Added includes for stage, types, lb vector helpers, JObj helpers, random, math, debug/assert, and related dependencies used by the new code.
Replaced a raw __assert block in grRCruise_801FF5B4 with HSD_ASSERT and changed allocation access to gp->u.map.chikuwa.
Bot report records 6 new full matches: grRCruise_80201988, grRCruise_80201288, grRCruise_80201110, grRCruise_801FF7A4, grRCruise_801FF2C8, and grRCruise_80200074.
Bot report records 11 improvements, including grRCruise_80200C04 to 90.59%, grRCruise_8020071C to 84.70%, grRCruise_80200154 to 91.81%, .data to 84.20%, and .sdata2 to 86.84%.

smart_moves.text: Moved grAnime_801C7BA0 into granime.h instead of keeping a source-local declaration in grrcruise, aligning the declaration with the owning grAnime interface.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: grAnime_801C7BA0 removed as static declaration in granime.c and added to granime.h

smart_moves.text: Used HSD_ASSERT for a recovered allocation assertion rather than leaving the expanded __assert form.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: grRCruise_801FF5B4 replaces __assert("grrcruise.c", 0x19A, "gp->u.map.chikuwa") with HSD_ASSERT(0x19A, gp->u.map.chikuwa)

smart_moves.text: Expressed repeated object/state handling as loops over fixed counts where the diff shows repeated grrcruise entry arrays.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: grRCruise_80200154 loops i < 3 over gp->gv.rcruise.x3C
diff_excerpt: grRCruise_80200C04 loops i < 17 over gp->gv.rcruise.entries

smart_moves.text: Kept the header/type surface moving with recovered body usage by changing types.h and grrcruise.h in the same PR as grrcruise.c.
evidence_refs: changed_files: grrcruise.c, grrcruise.h, types.h changed together

decomp_lessons.lesson: When recovered stage code calls a helper from another ground translation unit, prefer exporting the prototype from the owning header over leaving stale static/local declarations.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: grAnime_801C7BA0 prototype added to granime.h and removed from granime.c static declarations
diff_excerpt: grRCruise_80200154 calls grAnime_801C7BA0

decomp_lessons.lesson: Stage state machines with small fixed entry arrays can often be represented as natural for loops over typed entries plus switch states, rather than copied per-entry blocks.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: grRCruise_80200154 uses for (i = 0; i < 3; i++) with switch (entry->x00)
diff_excerpt: grRCruise_80200C04 uses for (i = 0; i < 17; i++) with switch (entry->x00)
decomp_standards:natural-loops
decomp_standards:canonical-control-flow-and-macros

decomp_lessons.lesson: Recovered grrcruise code benefits from typed Ground/GroundVars access for stage-specific state, but the slice does not include enough of types.h to audit every field name or union arm.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: many new accesses through gp->gv.rcruise, gp->gv.rcruise2, gp->u.scroll, gp->u.map.chikuwa
changed_files: types.h added 37 deleted 2
decomp_standards:typed-fields-over-pointer-math

decomp_lessons.lesson: Use existing assert macros when the recovered source line and expression match the macro form.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: raw __assert in grRCruise_801FF5B4 converted to HSD_ASSERT(0x19A, gp->u.map.chikuwa)
decomp_standards:assert-report-macros

naming_conventions.convention: Rainbow Cruise globals and functions retain the local grRc_/grRCruise_ address-style naming pattern, such as grRc_803E4DA8, grRc_803E4ECC, grRc_804D6A10, and grRCruise_80200154.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: added grRc_803E4DA8 and grRc_803E4ECC
diff_excerpt: recovered functions keep grRCruise_80xxxxxx names
decomp_standards:conservative-naming

naming_conventions.convention: Locals use short role names where clear, such as gp, gobj, jobj, archive, data, entry, dx, dy, and dist.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: grRCruise_801FF7A4 declares Ground* gp, HSD_JObj* jobj, UnkArchiveStruct* archive, DynamicsDesc* data
diff_excerpt: grRCruise_80200578 declares dx, dy, dist

naming_conventions.convention: Semantic names appear only for immediately local roles, while unclear globals and recovered struct fields remain address/offset-like, e.g. x0C, x14, x44.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: grRc_804D6A10 anonymous struct fields remain x0, x4, x8, x0C, etc.
decomp_standards:conservative-naming

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: PAD_STACK was added narrowly in several recovered functions to steer stack layout.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: PAD_STACK(8) in grRCruise_801FF7A4
diff_excerpt: PAD_STACK(0x8) in grRCruise_801FF924
diff_excerpt: PAD_STACK(0x18) in grRCruise_801FFADC
diff_excerpt: PAD_STACK(16) in grRCruise_80200578
text_corpus issue_comment: bot report shows grRCruise_801FF7A4 and grRCruise_80200074 became 100%, while grRCruise_801FF924 and grRCruise_80200578 improved but did not fully match
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: The PR slice has bot score evidence but no detailed objdiff rationale tying each PAD_STACK to an exact local assembly requirement.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used direct user_data/hsd_obj access in places instead of GET_GROUND/GET_JOBJ, apparently for code shape in grRCruise_801FF7A4.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: grRCruise_801FF7A4 introduces stage_gobj = gobj, gp = stage_gobj->user_data, jobj = stage_gobj->hsd_obj and removes GET_GROUND/GET_JOBJ
text_corpus issue_comment: grRCruise_801FF7A4 improved from 92.94% to 100.00%
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: Do not generalize this as a style rule; loaded standards prefer typed helpers/accessors when they match local evidence.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used ordinary C control flow for recovered state machines, including for loops, switch statements, ternary-like assignments, and modulo toggles.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: grRCruise_80200154 switch over entry->x00 with modulo checks
diff_excerpt: grRCruise_80200C04 switch over entry->x00 with HSD_Randi and state transitions
text_corpus issue_comment: grRCruise_80200154 improves to 91.81%, grRCruise_80200C04 improves to 90.59%

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Data-section progress was part of this PR, with explicit stage data and bot-reported .data/.rodata/.sdata2 improvements.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: adds grRc_803E4DA8 and grRc_803E4ECC
text_corpus issue_comment: .data improves from 16.99% to 84.20%, .sdata2 from 29.17% to 86.84%, .rodata from 0.00% to 77.78%
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: The slice does not include symbol metadata or split evidence beyond the diff and bot report.

The diff is truncated at about 33 KB; late hunks and full grrcruise.h/types.h content are not visible in loaded context.
Bot report supports match/improvement claims but does not provide per-line objdiff rationale or build command details.
No human reviewer feedback is available in the slice.
Merged state is available from metadata, but intake does not treat merge as proof that every tactic should become a global standard.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Export recovered cross-TU helper prototypes from the owning header
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2429 moved grAnime_801C7BA0 into granime.h and removed its stale static declaration from granime.c while grrcruise.c calls the helper. This is a concrete example of truthful header updates accompanying recovered body usage.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: grAnime_801C7BA0 added to granime.h
diff_excerpt: static grAnime_801C7BA0 declaration removed from granime.c
diff_excerpt: grRCruise_80200154 calls grAnime_801C7BA0

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use natural loop plus switch for fixed-size stage state arrays
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2429 recovers grrcruise state-machine code as loops over 3 subentries and 17 entries with switch statements on entry state fields, rather than repeated generated blocks.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: grRCruise_80200154 for i < 3 with switch(entry->x00)
diff_excerpt: grRCruise_80200C04 for i < 17 with switch(entry->x00)

accepted_candidate_records.title: PAD_STACK used in grrcruise matching, but retain narrow evidence
accepted_candidate_records.text: Several grrcruise functions added PAD_STACK while the bot report shows related functions matched or improved. Treat this as a local matching tactic example, not a broad style rule, because the slice lacks detailed per-function objdiff rationale.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: PAD_STACK in grRCruise_801FF7A4, 801FF924, 801FFADC, 80200074, 80200578
text_corpus issue_comment: bot report new matches and improvements

source_update_candidates.title: grrcruise is the Rainbow Cruise stage translation unit
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2429 evidence connects src/melee/gr/grrcruise.c with RCRUISE, /GrRc.dat, grRc_ symbols, and StageData grRc_803E4ECC.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: struct StageData grRc_803E4ECC = { RCRUISE, ..., "/GrRc.dat", ... }
metadata initial_classification systems: stage
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful path fact candidate, but should be curator-checked against current source/path facts before promotion.

source_update_candidates.title: Rainbow Cruise stage data symbols in PR 2429
source_update_candidates.text: The PR adds or references grRc_803E4DA8, grRc_803E4ECC, grRc_803E5014, grRc_803B8288, grRc_803E4FF0, grRc_804D4790, and grRc_804D6A10 in grrcruise.c.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: grRc_803E4DA8 S16Vec3 array
diff_excerpt: grRc_803E4ECC StageData

rejection_notes: Do not infer that direct user_data/hsd_obj access is globally preferred over GET_GROUND/GET_JOBJ; it is only evidenced as a local shape change in grRCruise_801FF7A4.
Do not promote the anonymous grRc_804D6A10 field layout or xNN field names as final semantic names from this slice alone.
Do not claim all grrcruise recovered functions fully matched; bot report distinguishes 6 new full matches from 11 partial improvements.
Do not infer human reviewer intent or requested changes; no human review comments are present.
Do not treat data-section improvements as fully validated ownership facts without symbol/split evidence beyond the PR diff and bot report.
